page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u2a-c1m2.2xlarge is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine running on the KVM hypervisor. It is equipped with 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs, utilizing AMD EPYC processors running at a clock speed of 2.25 GHz. The system features a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration, meaning hyperthreading is disabled, providing dedicated execution resources per vCPU.

The instance is configured with 16.0 GB of system memory, resulting in a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include any bundled local NVMe or SSD storage, requiring external block storage attachment for operating system and data storage needs. There are no integrated graphics processing units on this instance. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 3 Gbps, and it does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Given the 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this instance is positioned as a compute-focused resource. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing performance without resource contention from other tenants. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring dedicated CPU cycles but modest memory footprints, such as batch processing, lightweight web serving, build systems, and distributed compute nodes.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u2a-c1m2.2xlarge is an x86_64 compute-focused virtual machine running on the KVM hypervisor. Powered by an AMD EPYC processor operating at 2.25 GHz, the instance provides 8 dedicated vCPUs across 8 physical cores with a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration. It features 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 2.0 GB per core resource density. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, relying on external network storage. It offers a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps and does not provide complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The dedicated CPU allocation makes it suitable for compute-bound workloads with moderate memory requirements, such as batch processing, build servers, and web application hosting.
